figures.caption: Table 1: Summary of the four Aplysia neuronal spiking datasets and stimulation protocols.

Figure 1: Number of recorded spikes per minute for each neuron across the four Aplysia datasets, with corresponding mean and median values across all neurons for each recording. Intervals shaded grey correspond to censored periods with no observation.

Figure 2: Example of intensity function λ(t) in [0, 50] for an HEP with delayed scaled exponential excitation function, changepoints at τ1 = 5, τ2 = 15, τ3 = 20, τ4 = 40, and scaled exponential inhibition functions α(t), β(t) and δ(t), with parameters λ0 = 0.1, θ0,α = 0.25, ηα = 0.2, ξα = 0.1, θ0,β = 0.4, ηβ = 0.3, ξβ = 0.05, θ0,δ = 1.5, ηδ = 1.0, ξδ = 0.15.
Figure 3: Histogram of estimated parameters, corresponding medians across all S = 1,000 simulations, and true values for the synthetic data experiment described in Section 4.1.
Figure 4: Estimated intensity functions λ(·) and hyperparameter functions α(·) and β(·) across all S = 1,000 simulations, for the synthetic data experiment described in Section 4.1.

Figure 5: Estimated intensity functions λ(·) and standard deviation across all S = 1,000 simulations with parameters estimated on the training period [0, 75], and predicted intensities in the test period [75, 100], for the synthetic data experiment described in Section 4.1.
Figure 6: Group-specific intensity functions for the synthetic data experiment described in Section 4.2, and resulting ARIs and estimated number of groups for S = 100 simulations.

Figure 7: Fitted intensity functions and histograms of recorded spikes for six neurons in the Aplysia data, under a HEP process with excitation function as in Equation (3), with hyperparameter functions as in Equations (4) and (5). Note: y-axes are on different scales, and bin width for the histograms was set to one minute.

Figure 8: Average number of recorded spikes per minute and estimated intensity functions for the groups obtained via the HEP-based clustering with excitation function as in Equation (3), with hyperparameter functions as in Equations (4) and (5), applied to the June 2023 Aplysia data. Note: y-axes are on different scales.
